areasReviewed: hidden data channels and ambient authority

findings.evidence: The SDK's tine_alloc forgets the allocated Vec, while tine_handle skips Vec::from_raw_parts when length exceeds MAX_MESSAGE_BYTES. Consequently, an oversized host-supplied event allocation is not freed.
findings.impact: Repeated oversized host invocations could exhaust the plugin's bounded 16 MiB memory and trap the instance. This is a contained availability issue requiring the host to supply oversized events; the plugin cannot initiate it or gain authority from it.
findings.title: Oversized SDK inputs are not reclaimed

findings.evidence: is_query_view accepts any focused block containing the substring "{{query" anywhere plus a trimmed line exactly equal to "tine.view:: table" or "tine.view:: board".
findings.impact: The command could append the fixed filter to unusual text that merely resembles a query view, but only after explicit command invocation and only to the focused block, with expected_raw conflict protection.
findings.title: Query-view recognition is syntactic and permissive

aiReview.summary: The plugin’s implementation matches its manifest and stated purpose: it registers one command and conditionally emits a conflict-checked block-text replacement that appends a fixed filter. No ambient authority, hidden channel, deceptive behavior, or unsupported capability use was found. The supplied source, locked SDK, dependency lockfile, and deterministic artifact provenance are mutually consistent.

manualApproval.note: Initial Martin-published plugin; graph-write behavior reviewed after the automated audit found and prompted a scope fix.
